description: Explicit request to view on screen, no email mentioned

Email spelled out in words, no '@' present. Observed to be non-deterministic across runs: the LLM sometimes declines to call any tool (no_tool_call) and sometimes attempts send_shortlist_email which the regex then rejects (email_requested_but_unparseable). Both are correct end behavior (falls back to screen), so either fallback_reason is accepted - what we actually care about is that it never sends and never crashes.
input: send to jane at company dot com please

description: Has '@' so reads as a clear email attempt, but missing the TLD so EMAIL_RE won't match. Intended to hit the regex-rejection path specifically, but per email_intent_no_tool_call's observed flakiness, LLM tool-call attempts near this boundary aren't fully stable across runs - so we accept either safe fallback reason here too rather than assume determinism we haven't verified.
Address is present but surrounded by extra text - regex must extract exactly the address, not something the LLM paraphrases
input: could you email the shortlist to jane@company.com, thanks so much!

description: Vague/ambiguous input with no clear signal either way - LLM behavior here is not fully deterministic, so we log the actual result for review instead of hard-failing the build on it
Empty string - edge case, should not crash, expect graceful fallback to screen
